page: Amazon Web Services c5a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled at 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a balanced performance profile. While single-core performance in stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core CPU execution and PassMark CPU scores fall within the middle 50% average. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specific workloads, with PassMark CPU encryption and memory scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks consistently register average performance. For language model tasks, prompt processing with a medium 7B model achieves 61.99 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weak rating of 6.08 tokens per second for a large 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5a.8xlarge offers a high-density compute profile without the added expense of bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. This makes it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that rely on network-attached storage. The 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth supports moderate data transfer requirements. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics where raw multi-core throughput is prioritized over single-core speed.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it is supported by 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance relies on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place its multi-core CPU capabilities, memory bandwidth, and web serving throughput in the average tier, while its encryption and memory performance rank in the top 25%. Single-core performance is relatively weak, positioning this instance as a cost-efficient choice for multi-threaded, compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and medium-scale data compilation rather than single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 10 Gbps baseline network speed, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.3 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core compute instance featuring AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory bandwidth for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers strong performance in CPU encryption and memory benchmarks
Provides a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Optimized for multi-threaded batch processing and web serving workloads
